backend/main.mo: Main Motoko actor defining user profile and emergency contact storage with role-based access control, exposing query and update methods for managing profiles and the emergency contact.
backend/migration.mo: Migration module that transforms the old actor state (with optional emergencyContact) to the new state (with required emergencyContact), defaulting to 'Aditya Patel' / '9546530546' if null.
feature_evidence.json: Feature evidence report mapping two implemented features—default emergency contact defaults and Home page emergency call display—to their relevant source files and test IDs.
frontend-file-summaries.txt: JSON document containing one-sentence summaries of all frontend source files, serving as a reference index for the frontend codebase.
frontend/index.css: Global CSS file importing Tailwind directives and defining OKLCH-based CSS custom properties for light and dark theme color tokens used throughout the app.
frontend/index.html: HTML entry point for the SafeGuard app with SEO meta tags, Open Graph/Twitter Card tags, JSON-LD structured data, and the root div for the React app.
frontend/public/robots.txt: Robots.txt allowing all crawlers full site access and pointing to the sitemap at /sitemap.xml.
frontend/public/sitemap.xml: XML sitemap listing the home and settings page URLs with priority and change frequency metadata for search engine indexing.
frontend/src/App.tsx: Root React component that configures TanStack Router with home and settings routes, wraps the app in ThemeProvider, and renders the Toaster notification component.
frontend/src/components/EmergencyCallMod: Modal dialog component that displays an emergency contact's name and number and provides a CALL NOW button that triggers a tel: protocol call.
frontend/src/components/Layout.tsx: Shared layout component rendering a sticky header with the SafeGuard brand and settings navigation button, a main content area, and a footer with attribution.
frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts: Custom hook that creates and caches an authenticated or anonymous ICP backend actor using React Query, reinitializing and invalidating dependent queries when the identity changes.
frontend/src/hooks/useEmergencyContact.t: Custom React Query hook that fetches the current emergency contact from the backend and exposes an updateContact mutation with automatic cache invalidation on success.
frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.t: Provides a React context and hook for Internet Identity authentication, managing login/logout state and persisting identity via AuthClient.
frontend/src/hooks/useUserProfile.ts: React Query hook that fetches and saves the current user's profile via the backend actor, with loading and mutation state.
frontend/src/hooks/useUserRole.ts: React Query hook that checks whether the current authenticated user is an admin by calling the backend actor.
frontend/src/index.css: Global Tailwind CSS stylesheet defining a safety-themed OKLCH color palette with light/dark mode variables and accessibility utilities.
frontend/src/main.tsx: Application entry point that mounts the React app wrapped with QueryClientProvider and InternetIdentityProvider.
frontend/src/pages/Home.tsx: Home page displaying an emergency helpline with a prominent call button and contact card, supporting both authenticated and unauthenticated users.
frontend/src/pages/Settings.tsx: Settings page allowing admin users to view and update the emergency contact name and phone number, with validation and auto-saving of user profiles.
frontend/src/utils/urlParams.ts: Utility functions for reading, persisting, and clearing URL parameters from both query strings and hash-based routes using sessionStorage.
frontend/tailwind.config.js: Tailwind CSS configuration with dark mode, custom OKLCH-based color tokens (primary, destructive, emergency, warning, chart, etc.), extended spacing/font/radius/shadow values, accordion keyframe animations, and plugins for typography, container queries, and animate.
project_state.json: Tracks project state (version 3) for a safety emergency contact website hardcoded with contact 'Aditya Patel' at 9546530546, listing existing features (home page call button, settings defaults) and pending backend default initialization request.
